subject: Engineering & Project Status Update — Week of April 24, 2026
introduction:
Dear Stakeholders,

Please find below your weekly engineering and project portfolio update. This period reflects a mixed status across active initiatives: one high-priority project has been rescheduled due to a third-party vendor issue, a critical revenue-gating initiative has been escalated to active development, and one client migration has been successfully closed. Immediate team focus has been realigned to protect a $200,000 enterprise deal opportunity.

sections.heading: Portfolio Snapshot
items: High priority: 2  |  In Progress: 1  |  Completed: 1  |  On Schedule: 0
Overall status: Mixed — action has been taken to address the two open high-priority items

sections.heading: Project Titan — High Priority | Delayed | 68% Complete | Revised Due Date: April 30, 2026
items: Project Titan has been rescheduled from April 15 to April 30, 2026.

Root cause: A third-party payment gateway vendor introduced a breaking API change without a sunset period, requiring a full rewrite of the integration module. This work is estimated at 40 additional engineering hours.
Beta testing has surfaced 12 outstanding P2 bugs that must be resolved prior to launch.
One engineer has been temporarily reassigned from Project Titan to SSO Expansion (see below), which is a contributing factor to the revised timeline.
The team is actively working to contain scope and meet the April 30 deadline.

sections.heading: SSO Expansion — High Priority | In Progress | 25% Complete | Due: End of Week
items: SSO Expansion has been escalated from backlog to active development after sitting overdue for 21+ days.
This initiative directly gates a $200,000 contract with enterprise prospect OmniCorp, who has stipulated SSO as a prerequisite for signing.
One engineer has been reallocated from Project Titan to accelerate delivery by end of this week.
Successful completion of SSO Expansion is expected to directly enable deal closure with OmniCorp.

sections.heading: Global Logistics Manual Migration — Medium Priority | Completed | 100% Complete
items: The Global Logistics Manual Migration has been completed successfully.
A Root Cause Analysis (RCA) addressing a prior service delay was delivered and accepted by the client.
The client is currently stable; however, they are closely monitoring future support response times.
The team is maintaining heightened SLA compliance and attentiveness to ensure continued client confidence.

sections.heading: Key Risks & Actions
items: RISK: Project Titan's revised April 30 deadline is contingent on resolving 12 P2 bugs and completing the payment gateway integration rewrite under reduced engineering capacity.
RISK: SSO Expansion remains overdue and is the single blocking factor for the OmniCorp deal. Engineering resources have been reallocated to address this immediately.
ACTION: Monitor Global Logistics client closely for SLA compliance — proactive communication is advised given their stated sensitivity to support response times.

conclusion:
The team has taken deliberate steps this week to protect our highest-impact commitments — most notably, reallocating resources to unblock the OmniCorp deal and establishing a realistic, achievable path to Project Titan's launch. We will continue to provide weekly updates as these initiatives progress. Please don't hesitate to reach out with any questions or requests for additional detail.

Best regards,
Project Management Office
